news.Title: The Dow Jones Industrial Average Is in Correction Territory: 2 Historically Cheap Components That Make for No-Brainer Buys Right Now
news.Content:
Volatility has returned to Wall Street. As of the closing bell on March 27, the stock market's most iconic index, the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) , has officially entered correction territory (down 10.01% from its all-time closing high). While the benchmark S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) hasn't reached correction territory, the Dow is being kept company by the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) , which is down 12.6% from its record high.

Although Iran war uncertainty , inflation fears, and artificial intelligence (AI) bubble worries are currently getting the better of investors, history shows that double-digit percentage declines in Wall Street's major indexes are phenomenal buying opportunities for patient investors.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Among the Dow's 30 components are two historically cheap, brand-name companies that make for no-brainer buys right now.

Visa

The first stock that makes for a genius buy amid the Dow Jones Industrial Average correction is credit-services provider Visa (NYSE: V) . Since the financial crisis, shares of Visa haven't declined by more than about a third from their all-time high. They're 21% below their record high, as of March 27.

The beauty of Visa's operating model is that it's not a lender. While some of its peers double-dip on both sides of a transaction, Visa's management has kept it solely focused on payment facilitation. This choice ensures that it doesn't have to set aside capital to cover loan losses or credit delinquencies when economic uncertainty arises. It also helps Visa bounce back from downturns faster than many of its peers.

Additionally, Visa's opportunity beyond the U.S. is tantalizing. Cross-border payment volume has been growing by double-digits and shows little sign of slowing. Whether through acquisitions or organic expansion, Visa has a lengthy runway to broaden its reach into underbanked foreign markets.

Lastly, Visa is historically inexpensive. Its forward price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of 20 represents a 25% discount to its average forward P/E over the trailing half-decade.

Microsoft

The second Dow component that makes for a no-brainer buy as Wall Street's ageless index sinks 10% from its record-closing high is tech titan Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) . Its shares have plunged 34% below their all-time high.

Story Continues

One of the more overlooked foundational puzzle pieces to Microsoft's success is its legacy operations, such as Windows and Office. While these segments aren't growing like they were earlier this century, they still generate exceptionally high margins and bountiful cash flow. In other words, these segments feed Microsoft's higher-growth initiatives.

Though cloud computing has been one of Microsoft's leading catalysts for years, it's the company's incorporation of generative AI solutions and large language model capabilities into Azure that's reaccelerating sales growth for one of its highest-margin segments. In Microsoft's fiscal second quarter (ended Dec. 31), constant-currency sales growth for the world's No. 2 cloud infrastructure services platform, Azure, clocked in at 38%.

It also doesn't hurt that Microsoft is swimming in cash. It closed out December with $89.5 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments, and is pacing more than $160 billion in net cash from operations for fiscal 2026.

Over the trailing five years, Microsoft has averaged a forward P/E of almost 30. You can buy shares right now for less than 19 times forecast earnings per share in fiscal 2027.

news.Title: 准了！台積熊本廠升級3奈米經部今給過 關鍵原因曝
news.Content:
准了！台積電董事長魏哲家2月初拜會日本首相高市早苗，送出熊本二廠晶從6奈米升級到3奈米大禮。經濟部投審會今天(31日)審議過關，原因是台灣去年已量產2奈米，而熊本廠3奈米要到2028年才投產，不違反最先進製程留台原則。

另外，台積電同時申報300億美金增資英屬維京群島 TSMC GLOBAL LTD，主要投資於銀行定期存款與美金債券，賺取孳息並降低外匯避險成本。

值得注意的是，這是護國神山歷年最大單筆匯出賺息避險投資。過去都是40億、100億美金左右，這次300億，已經相當9600億台幣，快到兆元。

台積電提報因應市場及客戶需求，日本熊本先進晶圓製造廠投資計畫的技術及產能，要變更為生產3奈米產品，月產能1萬5000片的12吋晶圓，預計自2028年進行設備安裝設定及量產。

經濟部投審司表示，台積電投資熊本廠只是做計畫變更，原本投資金額沒有改動。雖然台積電宣稱成投資金額會從122億美元提高至170億美元，但從台灣過去的投資沒變，增加金額部分會是日本政府補貼計畫一環。

至於給過理由，投審司說明，台灣去年已生產2奈米，而熊本廠3奈米是到2028年才投產，屆時台灣已經進入1.4奈米製程，沒有違反「最先進製程留台」原則，因此給審議會通過。

news.Title: 中東戰火延燒　外資3月賣超台股9682億元創紀錄
news.Content:
（中央社記者曾仁凱台北31日電）台股集中市場指數今天下跌795.17點，收在31722.99點，一舉跌破季線並失守32000點關卡。根據台灣證券交易所統計，外資3月在集中市場賣超約新台幣9682.13億元，創史上外資最大單月賣超金額紀錄，遠遠超越2025年3月的4656.22億元。

美伊戰火持續延燒，引發投資人擔憂，台股集中市場指數今天終場下跌795.17點，收在31722.99點，為台股史上第12大單日跌點，除跌破季線32181點，並失守32000點整數關卡，成交值7939.64億元。

觀察三大法人籌碼動向，外資及陸資（不含外資自營商）今天大舉賣超807.45億元，連4賣；自營商減碼210.16億元，僅投信站在買方，買超30.97億元；三大法人合計賣超986.64億元。

個股方面，外資今天賣超前3名為元大台灣50達7萬742張、大成鋼3萬4768張及主動統一台股增長2萬2376張。另外，外資今天賣超台積電1萬5959張，為賣超排行第9名。

買超個股方面，外資今天買超前3名為台化4萬1744張、南亞2萬7414張及中石化1萬1732張，皆為塑化族群。如果加上買超第5名的台聚及第7名的亞聚，今天外資買超榜前10名，塑化股就占5席。

國泰證期顧問處協理蔡明翰分析，現階段AI基本面沒有太大改變，但短線受中東局勢干擾，牽動油價、通膨等因素，加上台股相對於美股位階高太多，短線恐面臨修正壓力。

蔡明翰認為，現在戰事持續，建議投資人不必太過糾結於細節，例如美國總統川普講了一個利多或利空，就去追高或殺低，投資人得自己判斷這場戰爭對經濟的可能影響。

如果認為戰爭會拖很久影響經濟，那現在台股才剛跌破季線，還在相對高的位置，應該趕快減碼；但如果認為川普最後會見風轉舵，戰事不會拖很久，那台股走勢很可能像去年對等關稅的翻版，戰事稍微告一段落就迅速反彈。不過投資人得先撐過這段時間，避免殺在低點、錯失市場反彈機會。（編輯：張良知）1150331

news.Title: 《半導體》南亞科斥資11.47億元採購機器設備
news.Content: 【時報-台北電】南亞科(2408)公告取得機器設備一批，交易總金額折合新台幣約11億4741萬9192元，供生產使用。交易相對人為MURATA MACHINERY, LTD.。（編輯：沈培華）

news.Title: 台積電尾盤再中冷箭 0050正2分割後散戶全衝了｜Yahoo財經掃描
news.Content:
受中東局勢不確定性干擾，加上油價持續上揚影響，美股四大指數漲少跌多。道瓊工業指數小漲0.11%，那斯達克指數跌0.73%，標普500指數跌0.39%，費城半導體指數重挫4.23%。科技股普遍承壓，其中記憶體族群表現最弱，美光與Sandisk同步大跌，半導體設備股也明顯走弱，拖累整體科技股氣氛，台積電ADR也下跌3.13%；另外，鮑爾談話稍稍緩解升息疑慮，金價回升、油價則仍處高檔震盪。

亞股表現同樣偏弱，日股下跌1.58%，韓股下挫4.26%，上證指數跌0.80%，港股則小漲0.09%。

回到台股，加權指數今(31)日終場重挫795.17點，收31,722.99點，成交金額7,939.63億元；權王 台積電(2330) 收跌20元至1,760元，尾盤再爆出13,812張賣單，持續成為壓抑指數的關鍵權值股。盤面上電子權值股普遍走弱， 鴻海(2317) 、 台達電(2308) 同步承壓，記憶體與載板族群遭到明顯拋售；不過資金並未全面退場，ETF反而成為成交重心，多檔ETF成交放量，反映震盪盤中投資人透過ETF參與大盤波動；此外，受國際紙漿供給與運價題材帶動，造紙股逆勢走強，成為今日少數亮點。

📝盤點熱門股：主動統一台股增長、元大台灣50正2、凱基台灣TOP50

今日熱門成交股方面，前五名全數收黑，資金主要集中在ETF、面板與記憶體族群。 主動統一台股增長(00981A) 以46.44萬張高居成交王，受「主動式ETF股王」話題帶動，在大盤劇烈震盪下吸引買盤與調節賣壓同步湧現；同屬ETF的 元大台灣50正2(00631L) 、 凱基台灣TOP50(009816) 也同步擠進前五，其中00631L小跌，與分割後恢復交易、入手門檻降低帶動買賣熱絡有關，009816則在盤勢走弱之際，反映市場資金持續透過台股ETF進行布局與換手。

個股方面，面板股 群創(3481) 走弱但成交維持高檔，顯示震盪盤中短線資金仍反覆進出人氣股；記憶體股 華邦電(2344) 也名列前五，主要反映記憶體族群賣壓升溫、連假前市場偏向先行減碼，帶動成交明顯放大。

📌三大法人買賣超

三大法人今日合計賣超986.64億元，賣壓明顯加重。其中外資賣超807.45億元，仍是盤面最大提款來源；自營商也同步調節210.16億元，顯示在大盤重挫之際，法人偏向保守操作；僅投信逆勢站在買方，單日買超30.97億元。整體來看，今天台股籌碼面仍由外資與自營商聯手偏空主導。

✨本日議題股：造紙概念股

議題股則聚焦造紙類股，在大盤重挫逾656點、權值股全面疲弱之際，造紙族群逆勢走強，成為盤面少數亮點，其中 華紙(1905) 、 寶隆(1906) 一度雙雙攻上漲停，華紙更是延續短線強勢，3月以來股價累計飆升逾5成。這波買盤主要圍繞漲價題材發酵，除了日本大王製紙宣布4月起調漲衛生紙、面紙與廚房紙巾等產品價格、漲幅超過10%，市場也關注華紙4月起紙品售價續漲...... 點我看更多

👥台股Q1爆紅榜：

Yahoo股市App公布的2026年第一季「十大爆紅台股榜」與「五大爆紅ETF榜」，反映第一季市場關注焦點明顯圍繞題材與事件驅動展開。台股榜由 燿華(2367) 拿下冠軍，主要受惠SpaceX IPO話題帶動低軌衛星與面板供應鏈想像升溫； 可寧衛*(8422) 則因土方新制議題衝上第二。ETF方面則呈現布局策略更多元，由主動 復華未來50(00991A) 奪下榜首...... 點我看更多

🔭重大財經事件展望

周三國際財經事件，先看美國市場，焦點集中在3月ADP就業人數變化、2月零售銷售，以及3月ISM製造業PMI，另有聖路易聯準銀行總裁穆薩勒姆發表演說；歐洲方面，歐元區將公布失業率，觀察就業市場是否延續穩定表現；亞太區則聚焦日本第一季短觀報告，以及日本、韓國、澳洲、印度3月製造業PMI終值，同時韓國也將公布3月進出口數據，有助掌握區域製造業與出口復甦力道；港澳方面，中國將發布3月製造業PMI，香港則公布3月外匯存底與2月零售銷售年增率，作為觀察內需、資金動能與陸港景氣的重要指標。

台灣方面，國內焦點包括中華經濟研究院發布3月台灣採購經理人指數、仲量聯行公布第一季商用不動產市場季報，另有華航皮卡丘彩繪機CI2發表、Ford與Peugeot新車發表等產業與消費話題可留意；企業動態則以法說會與股東會為主，股東會可關注 宏羚(7885) ，法說會則挑幾家重要公司觀察，包括 聚陽(1477) 、 大亞(1609) 、 三商電(2427) 、 王道銀行(2897) 、 三能-KY(6671) 與 創為精密(6899) 等；此外， 大宇(1445) 、 世豐(2065) 、 上品(4770) 、 驊宏資(6148) 、 新代(7750) 、 櫻花(9911) 等個股除息，短線股價表現與資金流向也值得一併追蹤。

市場變化難捉摸？面對股市的大起大落，總會有看不懂、跟不上、難決策的時刻！ Yahoo財經掃描 每個台股交易日晚間6點上菜，整理市場最重要的財經熱點，從國際財經趨勢、產業焦點到個股異動，迅速掌握投資脈動，帶你快人一步，掌握市場先機。

註：加權指數成交金額含盤後定價，不含零股、鉅額、拍賣及標購

（核稿編輯：劉凱銘）

news.Title: 台積電尾盤再中冷箭 0050正2分割後散戶全衝了｜Yahoo財經掃描
news.Content:
受中東局勢不確定性干擾，加上油價持續上揚影響，美股四大指數漲少跌多。道瓊工業指數小漲0.11%，那斯達克指數跌0.73%，標普500指數跌0.39%，費城半導體指數重挫4.23%。科技股普遍承壓，其中記憶體族群表現最弱，美光與Sandisk同步大跌，半導體設備股也明顯走弱，拖累整體科技股氣氛，台積電ADR也下跌3.13%；另外，鮑爾談話稍稍緩解升息疑慮，金價回升、油價則仍處高檔震盪。

亞股表現同樣偏弱，日股下跌1.58%，韓股下挫4.26%，上證指數跌0.80%，港股則小漲0.09%。

回到台股，加權指數今(31)日終場重挫795.17點，收31,722.99點，成交金額7,939.63億元；權王 台積電(2330) 收跌20元至1,760元，尾盤再爆出13,812張賣單，持續成為壓抑指數的關鍵權值股。盤面上電子權值股普遍走弱， 鴻海(2317) 、 台達電(2308) 同步承壓，記憶體與載板族群遭到明顯拋售；不過資金並未全面退場，ETF反而成為成交重心，多檔ETF成交放量，反映震盪盤中投資人透過ETF參與大盤波動；此外，受國際紙漿供給與運價題材帶動，造紙股逆勢走強，成為今日少數亮點。

📝盤點熱門股：主動統一台股增長、元大台灣50正2、凱基台灣TOP50

今日熱門成交股方面，前五名全數收黑，資金主要集中在ETF、面板與記憶體族群。 主動統一台股增長(00981A) 以46.44萬張高居成交王，受「主動式ETF股王」話題帶動，在大盤劇烈震盪下吸引買盤與調節賣壓同步湧現；同屬ETF的 元大台灣50正2(00631L) 、 凱基台灣TOP50(009816) 也同步擠進前五，其中00631L小跌，與分割後恢復交易、入手門檻降低帶動買賣熱絡有關，009816則在盤勢走弱之際，反映市場資金持續透過台股ETF進行布局與換手。

個股方面，面板股 群創(3481) 走弱但成交維持高檔，顯示震盪盤中短線資金仍反覆進出人氣股；記憶體股 華邦電(2344) 也名列前五，主要反映記憶體族群賣壓升溫、連假前市場偏向先行減碼，帶動成交明顯放大。

📌三大法人買賣超

三大法人今日合計賣超986.64億元，賣壓明顯加重。其中外資賣超807.45億元，仍是盤面最大提款來源；自營商也同步調節210.16億元，顯示在大盤重挫之際，法人偏向保守操作；僅投信逆勢站在買方，單日買超30.97億元。整體來看，今天台股籌碼面仍由外資與自營商聯手偏空主導。

✨本日議題股：造紙概念股

議題股則聚焦造紙類股，在大盤重挫逾656點、權值股全面疲弱之際，造紙族群逆勢走強，成為盤面少數亮點，其中 華紙(1905) 、 寶隆(1906) 一度雙雙攻上漲停，華紙更是延續短線強勢，3月以來股價累計飆升逾5成。這波買盤主要圍繞漲價題材發酵，除了日本大王製紙宣布4月起調漲衛生紙、面紙與廚房紙巾等產品價格、漲幅超過10%，市場也關注華紙4月起紙品售價續漲...... 點我看更多

👥台股Q1爆紅榜：

Yahoo股市App公布的2026年第一季「十大爆紅台股榜」與「五大爆紅ETF榜」，反映第一季市場關注焦點明顯圍繞題材與事件驅動展開。台股榜由 燿華(2367) 拿下冠軍，主要受惠SpaceX IPO話題帶動低軌衛星與面板供應鏈想像升溫； 可寧衛*(8422) 則因土方新制議題衝上第二。ETF方面則呈現布局策略更多元，由主動 復華未來50(00991A) 奪下榜首...... 點我看更多

🔭重大財經事件展望

周三國際財經事件，先看美國市場，焦點集中在3月ADP就業人數變化、2月零售銷售，以及3月ISM製造業PMI，另有聖路易聯準銀行總裁穆薩勒姆發表演說；歐洲方面，歐元區將公布失業率，觀察就業市場是否延續穩定表現；亞太區則聚焦日本第一季短觀報告，以及日本、韓國、澳洲、印度3月製造業PMI終值，同時韓國也將公布3月進出口數據，有助掌握區域製造業與出口復甦力道；港澳方面，中國將發布3月製造業PMI，香港則公布3月外匯存底與2月零售銷售年增率，作為觀察內需、資金動能與陸港景氣的重要指標。

台灣方面，國內焦點包括中華經濟研究院發布3月台灣採購經理人指數、仲量聯行公布第一季商用不動產市場季報，另有華航皮卡丘彩繪機CI2發表、Ford與Peugeot新車發表等產業與消費話題可留意；企業動態則以法說會與股東會為主，股東會可關注 宏羚(7885) ，法說會則挑幾家重要公司觀察，包括 聚陽(1477) 、 大亞(1609) 、 三商電(2427) 、 王道銀行(2897) 、 三能-KY(6671) 與 創為精密(6899) 等；此外， 大宇(1445) 、 世豐(2065) 、 上品(4770) 、 驊宏資(6148) 、 新代(7750) 、 櫻花(9911) 等個股除息，短線股價表現與資金流向也值得一併追蹤。

市場變化難捉摸？面對股市的大起大落，總會有看不懂、跟不上、難決策的時刻！ Yahoo財經掃描 每個台股交易日晚間6點上菜，整理市場最重要的財經熱點，從國際財經趨勢、產業焦點到個股異動，迅速掌握投資脈動，帶你快人一步，掌握市場先機。

註：加權指數成交金額含盤後定價，不含零股、鉅額、拍賣及標購

（核稿編輯：劉凱銘）

news.Title: Got $3,000? 2 Cloud Stocks That Wall Street Analysts Raised Targets on This Month.
news.Content:
Cloud computing stocks have been an interesting case this year. Although demand for cloud computing continues to surge, many of the stocks in this industry have sputtered.

One of the reasons is the stocks have become too expensive and overvalued after three years of bull market gains. But there are other reasons specific to cloud stocks. Many are dealing with capacity constraints to handle the high demand, which has hurt their ability to maximize profits.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Also, cloud computing stocks are spending a ton of money, more than they have in the past, on building out their AI infrastructure to handle the capacity constraints. However, many investors are questioning whether these enormous capital expenditures are going to produce adequate returns or just eat into free cash flow, compress margins, and stunt earnings. Others contend that the infrastructure investments are necessary for long-term growth.

In the analyst community, there have been some bullish calls on cloud stocks this month. Here are two cloud stocks that recently were upgraded by Wall Street stock pickers.

1. Arm Holdings

Arm Holdings (NASDAQ: ARM) is not a cloud computing company, but it serves that industry by providing the designs and licensing them to chipmakers to build their own chips. It is dominant in smartphone chips and has been expanding in the AI data center market.

But earlier this week, Arm announced a new direction for the company, launching its own CPU chip, the Arm AGI CPU, which is built for data centers to handle agentic AI workloads. Meta Platforms has signed on to use the chips along with other customers including Cerebras, Cloudflare , OpenAI, F5 Positron, Rebellions, SAP , and SK Telecom .

This new business would be in addition to its chip designing business. At the March 24 Arm Everywhere event, management projected huge growth from the new chips. Arm is calling for the chips to generate $15 billion annually starting in 2031, raising total revenue to $25 billion and earnings to $9 per share. For comparison, the company is anticipating revenue of about $5 billion this fiscal year.

Analysts at Needham upgraded Arm stock to a buy on the news and set a price target of $200 per share. The stock is currently trading at about $138 per share (as of March 30), so that would be 45% upside for Arm stock.

"We have been on the sidelines on ARM for 2.5 years and now see a series of its high-stakes bets, including raising royalty rates, going into subsystems, and making its own silicon, are working," Needham analysts wrote. "These bold moves should disrupt the existing industry landscape, one of the reasons why we were cautious, but are now transforming ARM for the better in a surprising way. With the rise of agentic AI and the growing role of CPU in AI data centers, we think ARM has become a credible AI play."

Story Continues

While the growth potential is huge, Arm stock is overvalued at 61 times forward earnings. But maybe that will come down after these new growth projections are factored in.

2. CrowdStrike

CrowdStrike (NASDAQ: CRWD) , the cloud-based cybersecurity company, got a significant upgrade from analysts at Morgan Stanley earlier in March. They bumped the rating for CrowdStrike stock to a buy, or overweight, and raised the price target to $510 per share, from $487.

At its current price of $384 per share, the new price target would indicate a 33% return during the next 12 months.

In their assessment, Morgan Stanley analysts said CrowdStrike is positioned to generate 20% annual revenue growth during the next few years. Morgan Stanley noted the growth potential of its Falcon Flex platform, which lets customers bundle services, as opposed to buying them separately. The Flex platform saw annual recurring revenue (ARR) jump 120% in the fiscal fourth quarter.

CrowdStrike also rolled out its Charlotte AI AgentWorks Ecosystem, which allows customers to use CrowdStrike's development platform and AI models to build their own custom security agents. Amazon , Nvidia , Accenture , Anthropic, Deloitte, Kroll, OpenAI, and Salesforce are among its customers and partners.

CrowdStrike reported fiscal fourth-quarter earnings (ended Jan. 31) in March, with revenue up 23% and overall ARR rising 24%.

While noting that CrowdStrike stock is expensive, Morgan Stanley analysts identified CrowdStrike as "best able to outperform in coming years."

CrowdStrike is trading at about 84 times forward earnings, so it is expensive. But Wall Street is bullish on these two and sees high earnings potential for both. Should you invest in them? It depends on how comfortable you are with those high valuations. It would be preferable to see updated forecasts or revenue projections from the new initiatives at both companies.

news.Title: 《台北股市》三大法人31日賣超前十大 電傳遭棄 塑化2檔、鴻海淪前五
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股31日加權指數收31722.99點，跌795.17點或2.45%，總成交值8317.06億元。三大法人賣超986.64億元，其中外資賣超807.46億元，投信、自營商分別買超30.98億元及賣超210.17億元。

三大法人賣超前十大個股，依類別分，4檔電子股、1檔金融股及5檔傳產股；依股價分，2檔百元股、1檔中價位股及7檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，10檔賣超上萬張，榜首為南亞(1303)，賣超3萬2122張，台化(1326)、群創(3481)名列二、三名，分別賣超2萬3245張及賣超2萬2204張。第四及第五名為仁寶(2324)、鴻海(2317)，其他依序為台新新光金(2887)、華航(2610)、台積電(2330)、台泥(1101)及台聚(1304)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為台積電(2330)286.72億元、鴻海(2317)35.90億元、南亞(1303)23.74億元。

●三大法人31日賣超前十名：

1.南亞(1303)收73.90元，跌7.50元或9.21%，成交量11.54萬張，三大法人賣超3萬2122張、估23.74億元，由前一交易日買超3萬5617張轉為賣超。

2.台化(1326)收44.95元，跌3.70元或7.61%，成交量13.08萬張，三大法人賣超2萬3245張、估10.45億元，由前一交易日買超4萬2788張轉為賣超。

3.群創(3481)收23.85元，跌0.75元或3.05%，成交量30.52萬張，三大法人賣超2萬2204張、估5.30億元，由前一交易日買超3728張轉為賣超。

4.仁寶(2324)收26.95元，跌1.30元或4.60%，成交量6.20萬張，三大法人賣超2萬1938張、估5.91億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超5萬8811張、估16.37億元。

5.鴻海(2317)收187.50元，跌6.50元或3.35%，成交量8.96萬張，三大法人賣超1萬9145張、估35.90億元，為連4日賣超，累計賣超3萬9957張、估76.71億元。

6.台新新光金(2887)收23.15元，跌0.60元或2.53%，成交量8.36萬張，三大法人賣超1萬9102張、估4.42億元，由前一交易日買超4670張轉為賣超。

7.華航(2610)收17.90元，跌0.45元或2.45%，成交量3.70萬張，三大法人賣超1萬6597張、估2.97億元，為連5日賣超，累計賣超3萬4111張、估6.23億元。

8.台積電(2330)收1760.00元，跌20.00元或1.12%，成交量5.84萬張，三大法人賣超1萬6291張、估286.72億元，為連4日賣超，累計賣超4萬6674張、估834.26億元。

9.台泥(1101)收23.00元，漲0.45元或2.00%，成交量4.06萬張，三大法人賣超1萬1839張、估2.72億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超1萬9038張、估4.35億元。

10.台聚(1304)收18.25元，漲0.45元或2.53%，成交量11.35萬張，三大法人賣超1萬1260張、估2.06億元，由前一交易日買超5886張轉為賣超。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

1.南亞(1303)三大法人累計買超3萬264張，其中外資買超3萬2555張、投信賣超5997張、自營商買超3706張；融資減5938張、融券增1306張，券資比2.74%。

2.台化(1326)三大法人累計買超5萬3914張，其中外資買超5萬3328張、投信賣超248張、自營商買超834張；融資減234張、融券增347張，券資比1.41%。

3.群創(3481)三大法人累計賣超5萬4316張，其中外資賣超5萬1151張、投信賣超440張、自營商賣超2723張；融資增875張、融券增1249張，券資比0.40%。

註：台股總成交值不含鉅額交易；個股買賣超僅31日未加計鉅額交易；資券統計截至前一交易日。（編輯：沈培華）

news.Title: York Space and Firefly Are Riding SpaceX's Coattails. Smaller Space Stocks Post Strong Gains.
news.Content:
Last Wednesday was a great day to be a space investor. (Thursday and Friday, somewhat less so!)

Rumors that SpaceX would file its IPO prospectus , which turned out to be false, set space stocks on fire. Shares of recent space IPOs York Space Systems (NYSE: YSS) and Firefly Aerospace (NASDAQ: FLY) shot up 5.1% and an astounding 16%, respectively, on Wednesday.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

The enthusiasm didn't last long, however. By Thursday, York had given back all of Wednesday's gains. (York did begin rising again Friday, however.) Firefly dropped only 2.6% on Thursday -- but then sold off 11.6% on Friday.

SpaceX's coattails, it would appear, were shorter than many space investors had hoped.

Hope springs eternal

Or perhaps investors were simply too impatient? After all, whispered among all the cheering about the possibility that SpaceX would maybe file for its IPO last week was the caveat that it might hold off until this week instead. If I had to make a wager, I'd bet we'll actually see the SpaceX prospectus -- and have a chance to invest in SpaceX -- in relatively short order.

That won't necessarily be good news for York and Firefly, however.

SpaceX versus York versus Firefly

Consider what we know about these three space stocks. Although the lack of a prospectus means we're still guesstimating at this point, most analysts agree SpaceX is probably a $16 billion-a-year company earning about $3 billion in annual profit. It's a space giant with its fingers in multiple space pies, from rockets to satellites to artificial intelligence (orbital data centers).

York Space is a much smaller operation. Valued at $2.7 billion in market capitalization, it's a rounding error next to SpaceX's rumored $1.75 trillion market cap. Focused primarily on building satellites, York boasts only $386 million in annual profit but is losing $84.5 million per year and burning $130 million in negative free cash flow.

Firefly stock is a bit bigger than York at $3.7 billion in market cap, but Firefly's business is smaller. Revenue clocks in just under $160 million. Annual losses topped $334 million last year. Negative free cash flow was $238 million.

Take away the names. Forget about the popularity factor. In any contest between an anonymous small, unprofitable, cash-burning company and a profitable rival 400 to 600 times its size, who do you think will win? Which stock would you prefer to own?

Story Continues

SpaceX will crush small space stocks

This is my big worry about the SpaceX IPO, at least in the near term. Over the long term, yes, history is replete with stories of small companies that beat the odds and grow to defeat their larger rivals. But in the short term, I expect many investors will weigh their options and decide they'd rather own SpaceX stock than York or Firefly -- or anyone else in space, really. I worry investors will instead sell these other stocks to buy SpaceX stock.

Far from providing coattails for its rivals to ride, my hunch is the SpaceX IPO will pull the rug out from under them.

news.Title: 外媒點台積電有望衝「3兆美元俱樂部」！揭沒怕2關鍵防火：已準備好
news.Content:
財經中心／綜合報導

美國投資媒體點名台積電（TSMC）有望躋身「3兆美元俱樂部」，與輝達（Nvidia）、蘋果（Apple）、Alphabet等科技巨頭並列，分析即便台積電面臨地緣政治風險升溫、中東局勢動盪，台積電仍握有人工智慧AI與先進製程兩大「關鍵戰力」，成為市場看好其長期成長的重要原因。

AI需求爆發，帶動先進晶片與資料中心市場成長，其中台積電掌握約71%晶圓代工市占，技術領先全球。（圖／民視資料照）

AI需求爆發 台積電成最大受益者

隨著AI快速發展，高效能運算與資料中心需求持續升溫，半導體產業的重要性再度被放大。美媒分析指出，台積電身為全球最大晶圓代工廠，在先進製程領域具備領先優勢，客戶涵蓋多家全球頂尖科技公司，因此被視為AI浪潮下最直接受益的企業之一。報導提到，台積電目前市值約1.6兆美元，若未來順利突破3兆美元門檻，股價仍具成長空間。尤其隨著AI晶片需求爆發，資料中心建設持續擴大，市場普遍認為台積電已站穩產業核心地位。

先進製程市占領先 科技巨頭高度依賴

分析指出，台積電在全球晶片代工市場市占率約71%，並生產超過90%的最先進半導體，技術門檻與競爭優勢明顯。包含蘋果、輝達、 AMD、Arm等科技大廠，都仰賴台積電生產次世代晶片，使其在AI、高效能運算與智慧型手機晶片市場維持關鍵地位。從營收結構來看，高效能運算、AI與資料中心相關晶片已占台積電營收約55%，智慧型手機晶片則占32%，顯示先進製程已成為公司主要成長引擎。

最新財報顯示營收與獲利同步成長，毛利率持續提升。市場預估最快2029年，台積電有機會挑戰3兆美元市值。（圖／民視資料照）

news.Title: Why 1 Top Analyst Says Apple Is a Screaming Buy Right Now
news.Content:
Tech is a wreck. So far in 2026, the technology sector is the second-worst-performing sector in the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) . Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) , which ranks as the world's second-largest technology company by market cap , is among the laggards. Shares of the iPhone maker are down more than 10% year to date.

However, Wall Street remains generally bullish about Apple. The consensus 12-month price target for the stock reflects an upside potential of around 20%. Roughly 60% of the analysts who cover Apple rate it as a "buy" or a "strong buy."

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

At least one top Wall Street analyst believes that Apple is a screaming buy right now. Morgan Stanley 's (NYSE: MS) Erik Woodring thinks the stock can jump almost 28% from its current level over the next 12 months. Why is Woodring so optimistic about Apple's near-term prospects?

Time for an upgrade

Morgan Stanley recently released its AlphaWise Global Smartphone Survey. And the results bode well for Apple's growth in 2026 and into 2027. In particular, the Wall Street firm's survey found that the number of consumers in the U.S. and China who plan to upgrade to new iPhones over the next 12 months reached a record high.

This interest in upgrades isn't an industrywide phenomenon. Woodring expects Apple to be the only major global smartphone vendor that will gain market share this year. He thinks rivals using Alphabet 's (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) Android operating system could see significantly lower smartphone demand due to higher prices resulting from a severe imbalance between supply and demand for memory chips.

Apple faces the same memory shortage. However, Woodring believes that the company's impressive brand loyalty should allow it to weather the storm better than its top competitors.

Morgan Stanley's survey reinforced this optimism. It found that Apple's net switching rate rose to 11% -- a five-year high. The recent survey revealed that Apple was the only major smartphone brand with a positive net switching rate and the only brand with an improving rate.

Know when to fold 'em

Why are customers eager to upgrade to new iPhones? Many of them are motivated by "new and advanced features," according to the AlphaWise Global Smartphone Survey. Other driving factors include better device quality for newer iPhones and a broad base of customers eligible for upgrades.

Story Continues

Interestingly, though, the Apple Intelligence capabilities aren't the main attraction for customers intending to upgrade. The survey found that consumer willingness to pay for these generative AI features sank 11% year over year.

What are iPhone owners especially hankering for? Foldable iPhones. Morgan Stanley's survey revealed that 27% of current iPhone customers are "extremely interested" in buying a foldable iPhone. The number jumps to nearly 40% for customers in China.

To be sure, Morgan Stanley doesn't predict that 27% of Apple's iPhone users will scramble to buy a foldable iPhone when the new device launches (expected in fall 2026). However, demand for the upcoming version appears robust. Apple could expand the global foldable smartphone market by more than 2x within 18 months of the launch of its foldable iPhone, according to Morgan Stanley's projections. This could put the new product's potential annual revenue at up to $60 billion.

Quality deserves a premium

Apple isn't a value stock , by any stretch of the imagination. Its forward price-to-earnings ratio stands at 28.8. That makes Apple the second-most-expensive member of the so-called "Magnificent Seven" stocks, ahead of only Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) .

However, the iPhone ecosystem remains exceptionally sticky. Apple has greater pricing power than most companies. With significant pent-up demand for new iPhones and a major new form factor on the way, the stock doesn't look overly expensive right now. Quality deserves a premium.

news.Title: The Nasdaq Is Officially in a Correction. Here's What Investors Should Do, According to History.
news.Content:
The Nasdaq-100 index tracks the performance of the top 100 companies (by value) listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange, excluding banks and other financial institutions. A whopping 60% of the index's portfolio is parked in the technology sector alone, so it has a high degree of exposure to rapidly growing artificial intelligence (AI) companies like Nvidia .

As a result, the Nasdaq-100 often outperforms more diversified indexes like the S&P 500 , but it also experiences more volatility during periods of market turmoil. The index is currently in correction territory after declining by more than 10% from its record high, as investors digest spiking oil prices caused by the ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Investors can track the performance of the Nasdaq-100 by purchasing an exchange-traded fund (ETF) like the Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ: QQQ) , which holds the same stocks and maintains similar weightings. History suggests now might be a great time to swoop in and buy. Here's why.

The Middle East conflict is making a tough situation even worse

Over the long term, stock prices are driven by corporate earnings. If investors think companies will be making less money in the future, they might trim their exposure to the stock market today. Oil is a key input cost for every product that requires transportation by air, land, or sea, leaving consumers with higher prices not just at the gas pump, but also at the grocery store and at their favorite retailers.

Nvidia is the largest holding in the Nasdaq-100, with a weighting of 8.5%. It sells the world's best graphics processing units (GPUs) for data centers, which are the primary chips used in AI development. Most of its customers are corporate giants like Microsoft and Amazon , so you might think its sales are less exposed to spiking oil prices. But those companies draw a huge chunk of their revenue from consumers, so if they make less money, they are likely to buy fewer chips.

Therefore, the knock-on effects from the conflict in the Middle East threaten to impact almost every company in the Nasdaq-100.

This economic shock arrived at a very unfortunate moment. According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics' latest non-farm payrolls report, the U.S. economy lost a whopping 92,000 jobs in February. Further, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell thinks the private sector has created zero jobs over the last six months, after adjusting for overcounting and poor data collection methods. Adding a potential spike in inflation due to rising oil prices could make this situation significantly worse in the coming months, casting uncertainty over corporate earnings.

Story Continues

Tech stocks were already on shaky ground

Before the Middle East conflict started, tech investors were already digesting a potential slowdown in AI data center infrastructure spending from some companies. OpenAI, which created ChatGPT, lowered its capital expenditures (capex) outlook from $1.4 trillion to $600 billion through 2030, casting doubt over its ability to purchase as much computing capacity from cloud providers as it originally planned.

Microsoft has a $625 billion order backlog from customers who are waiting for more data centers to come online to power their AI development goals, and 45% (or $281 billion) is attributable to OpenAI alone. Similarly, reports suggest OpenAI has a whopping $300 billion deal with Oracle to buy computing capacity. These hefty numbers are now questionable.

In my opinion, even OpenAI's revised capex forecast of $600 billion over the next four years might be ambitious. The company only has $25 billion in annualized revenue right now, and it's losing a ton of money at the bottom line. It just raised $120 billion from investors, but that still doesn't come close to fulfilling its capex needs.

Here's what investors should do during the Nasdaq correction

With all of the headwinds I've detailed above, it's no surprise the Nasdaq-100 has plunged by over 10%. The question is whether a correction of this magnitude is representative enough of the risks ahead, but it's almost impossible to answer that today. If oil prices continue to climb, for example, further downside in the stock market is likely.

However, volatility is a normal part of the investing journey. The Nasdaq-100 has experienced five bear markets (declines of 20% or more) over the last 26 years alone, triggered by events like the bursting of the dot-com bubble in 2000, the global financial crisis in 2008, the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020, the inflation crisis in 2022, and the Trump administration's "Liberation Day" tariffs in 2025.

While it's impossible to predict exactly what the Nasdaq-100 will do in the short term, it has always climbed to new highs over the long run, so investors who bought dips have yielded very strong returns. In fact, the Invesco QQQ ETF has delivered a compound annual return of 10.3% since it was established in 1999, even after accounting for every sell-off, correction, and bear market along the way.

Although the AI boom appears to be hitting turbulence, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang thinks data center operators could spend up to $4 trillion per year on infrastructure by 2030 to meet astronomical demand for computing capacity from AI developers. Perhaps OpenAI won't be one of the long-term winners as many people thought, but there are enough companies competing for AI supremacy that overall spending probably won't suffer a sharp slowdown.

As a result, this sell-off could be a great opportunity for investors to buy some exposure to the Nasdaq-100 through the Invesco QQQ ETF, as long as they maintain a long-term outlook of five years or more.

news.Title: 台股逐洞賽｜空軍壓境2天來爆殺1,400點！法人：第二季準備上演史詩級V轉
news.Content:
中碳（1723）表現最佳：中碳產品售價與油價連動，但原料卻來自煤焦油體系，故油價上漲有望提升獲利。此外，BBU相關產品已進入驗證階段，預計下半年起逐步放量。

元晶（6443）表現最差：主因短線獲利了結賣壓。惟後市看好受惠SpaceX 6月IPO，營運可望重回成長。

盤勢預測：

今天是第一季度的最後一個交易日。受中東戰火延燒、科威特海水淡化廠遭到攻擊的消息衝擊，市場擔憂能源供應與區域穩定，引發避險情緒升溫，致使台股早盤開低震盪。

從盤面結構來看，記憶體股今日稍作休息，資金明顯流向具備防禦屬性與漲價題材支撐的傳統產業。其中，造紙類股成為今日市場最吸睛的焦點，包括華紙（1905）、寶隆（1906）等個股在買盤簇擁下表現強勢。

展望後市，本周即將面臨清明節四天連假，根據歷史經驗，長假前夕市場交投往往趨於審慎，觀望氣氛濃厚。目前台股技術面正處於關鍵位置，預估短線將維持在季線附近進行震盪整理。季線作為中長線的多空分水嶺，若能有效守穩，有利於節後多方重整旗鼓；反之，若跌破則需防範回測半年線的壓力。

更值得投資人關注的是資金面的動向。外資在3月份的賣超金額創下歷史新高，這無疑給大盤帶來了沉重的壓力。進入4月份後，外資的操作慣性是否會由賣轉買，抑或是持續賣超，將是決定台股能否發動「清明變盤」向上挑戰的關鍵指標。

總結來說，目前台股處於「內熱外冷」的交界點。操作策略上，建議投資人切勿過度追高，在清明長假前應保持適度的現金水位，降低融資槓桿。選股方向可關注首季財報表現優異、且外資開始回補的質優龍頭股。在地緣政治與通膨變數尚未完全消散前，控管風險、耐心等待季線附近的整理落底，才是現階段相對穩健的投資之道。

投組表現：

建漢（3062）表現最佳，31日股價下跌3.42%，股價在月線、季線之上，後市看好。

嘉聯益（6153）表現最差，31日股價下跌4.23%，股價跌破月、季線，停損賣出。

台股在美股半導體族群重挫拖累下出現明顯回檔，美國四大指數漲跌互見，但費城半導體指數大跌4.23%，科技股賣壓升溫，影響台股電子權值股同步走弱。加權指數終場重挫795點，跌幅達2.45%，收在31,722點，市場恐慌情緒升高，成交量也明顯放大。

從技術面觀察，加權指數已正式跌破季線重要支撐，代表中短期趨勢轉弱，市場由原本高檔整理轉為修正格局，在未重新站回季線之前，指數仍可能維持震盪偏空走勢。

面對盤勢轉弱，投資人應嚴守風險控管紀律，落實先前提醒的保守策略，包括降低持股水位、不追高強勢股以及避免資金過度集中，採取分散布局與提高現金比重方式因應市場波動，等待趨勢重新轉強後再行布局。

族群方面，石英元件、文創、手機鏡頭、車用鏡頭及工具機等概念股受大資金買進，股價表現相對抗跌。

同致（3552）

股本9.7

news.Title: Qnity (Q) Partners with NVIDIA to Advance Semiconductor R&D
news.Content:
Qnity Electronics, Inc. (NYSE: Q ) ranks among the recent spin-off companies that hedge funds are piling into . On March 18, Qnity Electronics, Inc. (NYSE:Q) announced a partnership with NVIDIA to advance materials research and development for semiconductors. The partnership aims to support studies on materials for next-generation AI, high-performance computing, and sophisticated packaging systems.

According to Randy King, Qnity’s Chief Technology and Sustainability Officer, using accelerated modeling enables the company to shorten development times and bring new tech to the market faster, while improving performance.

Moreover, on March 11, Qnity Electronics, Inc. (NYSE:Q) established a 385,000-square-foot manufacturing plant in Newark, Delaware. The facility features a new production line that manufactures components for chemical mechanical planarization (CMP) pads. The development is part of the company’s multi-year initiative to boost semiconductor manufacturing capacity.

Qnity Electronics, Inc. (NYSE:Q) manufactures electronic materials for the semiconductor and electronics industries, with its brands including Acuplane, Circuposit, Duroptix, EPIC, Ikonic, Kalrez, Kapton, Laird, Microfill, Pyralux, Riston, Solderon Tin, and Silveron Silver.

While we acknowledge the potential of Q as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock .

news.Title: Amazon Stock Investors Just Got Great News Concerning OpenAI and Robotaxis
news.Content:
Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) shares have fallen 20% from their high due in part to concerns about the company's plan to spend about $200 billion on capital expenditures this year. Also, recession fears have resurfaced because the U.S.-Iran war has pushed oil prices to a multiyear high. That has hurt consumer discretionary stocks like Amazon.

However, Amazon shareholders recently got some great news about the company's artificial intelligence and robotaxi businesses. Here are the important details.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Amazon expanded its partnership with OpenAI

In February, Amazon Web Services (AWS) and OpenAI announced an expanded partnership. Amazon plans to invest $50 billion in the San Francisco-based start-up, and OpenAI will spend $138 billion (including a previous $38 billion commitment) on AWS cloud services over the next eight years.

The expanded partnership includes OpenAI's commitment to consume about 2 gigawatts of Trainium capacity through AWS infrastructure. Trainium is a custom AI chip developed to accelerate model training workflows, though it has also been tuned to support inference. Anthropic already uses more than a million Trainium chips, so OpenAI's commitment affords Amazon's custom silicon even more credibility.

Additionally, Amazon and OpenAI will create a stateful runtime environment (SRE) powered by OpenAI models, including the models that power ChatGPT. The SRE will be available on Amazon Bedrock, a generative artificial intelligence development platform. Stateful means applications and agents built on the platform will remember context from previous conversations.

I make that distinction because Microsoft Azure holds exclusive rights to stateless OpenAI APIs (application programming interfaces). That means any stateless application or agent that accesses OpenAI models via API must run on Azure infrastructure. What's the difference? Stateless API calls are fine for simple tasks, but stateful API calls are necessary for multistep workflows.

"If your organization relies on standard API calls for content generation, summarization, or simple chat, Microsoft Azure remains the primary destination," writes VentureBeat. "If your goal is to build 'AI coworkers' that require deep integration with AWS-hosted data and persistent memory access across weeks of work, the AWS stateful runtime environment is the clear choice."

Story Continues

Here's why this matters: AWS reported 24% revenue in the fourth quarter, marking its fastest growth in over four years. With OpenAI models now available through Amazon Bedrock, revenue growth could continue accelerating the coming quarters.

Amazon's Zoox is coming to Austin and Miami

In 2020, Amazon acquired Zoox, an autonomous driving technology company founded in 2014. Last year, Zoox began offering free trips to the public in Las Vegas and San Francisco, and its robotaxis have since carried about 350,000 riders. This year, the company plans to expand its coverage area in both cities, and it will begin offering rides to a limited number of people in Austin and Miami.

Amazon's Zoox is a few years behind Alphabet 's Waymo, which provided more than 14 million trips in 2025 alone, and is on pace to deliver a million rides per week by the end of 2026. Additionally, Waymo already offers paid robotaxi services in 10 U.S. cities across Arizona, California, Florida, Georgia, and Texas.

Not only has Zoox served fewer riders across fewer cities, but also the company is still awaiting regulatory approval to charge for rides. On March 11, Zoox submitted an application to operate a commercial ride-sharing service with up to 2,500 robotaxis. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration will make a decision by April 10.

Looking ahead, Morgan Stanley analysts believe Zoox will account for 12% of autonomous vehicle trips per year by 2032. That would put the company in fourth place behind Waymo (34%), Tesla (25%), and Uber (22%). Morgan Stanley estimates robotaxis have an addressable market of more than $1 trillion in the U.S. alone.

Here's why this matters: Most investors are well aware that Amazon has a strong presence in e-commerce, digital advertising, and cloud computing, but Zoox could become a fourth major revenue stream over the next decade.

We just issued 'double down' alerts on 3 stocks — find out if Amazon made our list

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $434,524 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $47,376 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $503,861 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of March 23, 2026

Trevor Jennewine has positions in Amazon and Tesla. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, Tesla, and Uber Technologies. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

However, Amazon shareholders recently got some great news about the company's artificial intelligence and robotaxi businesses. Here are the important details.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Amazon expanded its partnership with OpenAI

In February, Amazon Web Services (AWS) and OpenAI announced an expanded partnership. Amazon plans to invest $50 billion in the San Francisco-based start-up, and OpenAI will spend $138 billion (including a previous $38 billion commitment) on AWS cloud services over the next eight years.

The expanded partnership includes OpenAI's commitment to consume about 2 gigawatts of Trainium capacity through AWS infrastructure. Trainium is a custom AI chip developed to accelerate model training workflows, though it has also been tuned to support inference. Anthropic already uses more than a million Trainium chips, so OpenAI's commitment affords Amazon's custom silicon even more credibility.

Additionally, Amazon and OpenAI will create a stateful runtime environment (SRE) powered by OpenAI models, including the models that power ChatGPT. The SRE will be available on Amazon Bedrock, a generative artificial intelligence development platform. Stateful means applications and agents built on the platform will remember context from previous conversations.

I make that distinction because Microsoft Azure holds exclusive rights to stateless OpenAI APIs (application programming interfaces). That means any stateless application or agent that accesses OpenAI models via API must run on Azure infrastructure. What's the difference? Stateless API calls are fine for simple tasks, but stateful API calls are necessary for multistep workflows.

"If your organization relies on standard API calls for content generation, summarization, or simple chat, Microsoft Azure remains the primary destination," writes VentureBeat. "If your goal is to build 'AI coworkers' that require deep integration with AWS-hosted data and persistent memory access across weeks of work, the AWS stateful runtime environment is the clear choice."

Story Continues

Here's why this matters: AWS reported 24% revenue in the fourth quarter, marking its fastest growth in over four years. With OpenAI models now available through Amazon Bedrock, revenue growth could continue accelerating the coming quarters.

Amazon's Zoox is coming to Austin and Miami

In 2020, Amazon acquired Zoox, an autonomous driving technology company founded in 2014. Last year, Zoox began offering free trips to the public in Las Vegas and San Francisco, and its robotaxis have since carried about 350,000 riders. This year, the company plans to expand its coverage area in both cities, and it will begin offering rides to a limited number of people in Austin and Miami.

Amazon's Zoox is a few years behind Alphabet 's Waymo, which provided more than 14 million trips in 2025 alone, and is on pace to deliver a million rides per week by the end of 2026. Additionally, Waymo already offers paid robotaxi services in 10 U.S. cities across Arizona, California, Florida, Georgia, and Texas.

Not only has Zoox served fewer riders across fewer cities, but also the company is still awaiting regulatory approval to charge for rides. On March 11, Zoox submitted an application to operate a commercial ride-sharing service with up to 2,500 robotaxis. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration will make a decision by April 10.

Looking ahead, Morgan Stanley analysts believe Zoox will account for 12% of autonomous vehicle trips per year by 2032. That would put the company in fourth place behind Waymo (34%), Tesla (25%), and Uber (22%). Morgan Stanley estimates robotaxis have an addressable market of more than $1 trillion in the U.S. alone.

Here's why this matters: Most investors are well aware that Amazon has a strong presence in e-commerce, digital advertising, and cloud computing, but Zoox could become a fourth major revenue stream over the next decade.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $47,376 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $503,861 !*

news.Title: Is Nvidia a Smart Buy for a Value Investor Right Now?
news.Content:
The hottest tech stocks of the times generally aren't on the buy list of value investors -- and this is because they often trade at lofty valuations. Excitement about the prospects of these players prompts investors seeking growth to rush into these names. We've seen this over the past few years with technology stocks focused on artificial intelligence (AI).

Companies from Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) to Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) saw their stock prices climb in the triple and quadruple digits, and valuation marched higher too.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

But, in recent weeks, these and other tech players have stumbled amid many concerns, from worries about the U.S. economy to the war in Iran. Investors have also expressed concern about the levels of spending on AI and whether this investment would bear significant fruit down the road.

All of this has weighed on the valuations of the world's top tech stocks -- including AI giant Nvidia. With Nvidia now trading at its lowest level in about a year, the stock is a buy. But is this top growth player even a smart buy for a value investor right now? Let's find out.

A classic growth stock

So, first, let's take a closer look at Nvidia. This player has been considered a growth stock for a good reason: It's been reporting explosive revenue gains, in the double and triple digits, quarter after quarter, during this AI boom. This is because Nvidia is the world's No. 1 designer of AI chips, selling the graphics processing units (GPUs) that everyone wants for their AI workloads. Nvidia's GPUs are the fastest around, and this is an important point for chip customers because speed can help them bring their AI platforms to market sooner -- and generate revenue more quickly.

Nvidia's leadership doesn't end with the GPU; the company has progressively added to its portfolio and offers networking tools, enterprise software, and now is expanding its reach into the area of AI agents. AI chips are needed to power these agents, so Nvidia already fits in here -- but the company is also taking other steps to play a key role in this growth area. For example, it recently announced NemoClaw, a platform that helps customers safely use the popular AI agent OpenClaw.

This chip designer's presence in AI helped revenue to climb to a record $215 billion in the latest full year, and as always over the past several quarters, gross margin has remained high at more than 70%. This shows that Nvidia is highly profitable on sales.

Story Continues

Nvidia's declining valuation

Though Nvidia's earnings are going strong, the company has still faced the headwinds brought on by market uncertainty in recent weeks. And that's resulted in a sharp decline in the company's valuation to levels often seen in the world of value stocks .

Value stocks are those that trade for less than what they're truly worth, and value investors specifically seek out these players with the idea that they eventually will reach their true value. This is a great strategy for long-term investors, because with an investment horizon spanning many years, they have time to watch this scenario play out. (But whether you favor growth or value stocks, long-term investing always is the way to go -- it allows your portfolio to recover from any bumps along the investing path and maximize gains.)

Revenue expectations

So, is Nvidia a value buy? Right now, yes, Nvidia could make the cut. Analysts expect annual revenue to climb 72% in the current full year, making the stock look undervalued at today's levels.

And evidence shows Nvidia could reach these growth expectations. The company continues to note high demand for its AI products, and the release of its next platform, Vera Rubin, later this year should offer it an extra boost. Comments from its customers, tech giants such as Meta Platforms and Amazon , support this idea of ongoing growth as they are investing to ramp up their AI infrastructure -- and as part of that, they need chips.

The one thing to note is that Nvidia, though it's wearing the cloak of a value player today, remains a growth stock, as its growth rate shows. And it's very likely the stock will return to a higher valuation in the future. So it may not offer the slower and steadier pace of a classic value stock, meaning it's not the best bet for every value investor.

But for value investors who like the idea of getting in on potentially explosive growth for a dirt cheap price, Nvidia makes a smart buy today.

news.Title: 三大法人砍「它」13萬張 網一票問該停損？ 專家：斷頭潮快出籠
news.Content:
受中東局勢影響，台股今（31）日開低走低，下跌795點，跌破3萬2關卡，終場以31722點作收，記憶體類股更是重災區，南亞科、旺宏被打到跌停，力積電、華邦電也有8.6%及3%的跌幅。對此，財經作家狄驤表示，今日中小型股開低走低，已經能聞到恐慌賣壓的味道，再這樣殺一兩天，融資斷頭潮就可能出籠。雖然他主觀看好AI發展，但還是會減碼多單。

記憶體大廠南亞科今日開低走低，一路下挫至跌停，股價跌破200元來到198.5元，即將創今年以來的新低；從籌碼面來看，自今年三月以來，三大法人合砍13萬張。對此，狄驤在臉書表示，最近社群平台上不少人在問南亞科要不要停損，即使對記憶體有信心，但帳面虧損已經快讓他們承受不住，甚至還有人是開融資，擺到今天應該已經被斷頭。

狄驤提到，今日中小型股開低走低，已經能聞到恐慌賣壓的味道，「也就是散戶的人踩人多殺多」，再這樣殺一兩天，融資斷頭潮就可能出籠。

狄驤指出，他日前就曾跟大家分享，美股頭部成形是積極空方訊號，就算他主觀看好AI發展，還是會減碼多單，因為型態被破壞是客觀事實，該減碼時就要盡快處理。

狄驤說明，若不減碼處理，就很容易在人踩人的時候殺低，劇烈去槓桿時，反而是嘗試站買方的好機會。

（封面示意圖／AI生成）

news.Title: Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller Piled Into 2 of the Hottest AI Stocks for a Second Consecutive Quarter (No, Not Nvidia or Palantir)
news.Content:
Data is the fuel that keeps Wall Street's engine turning. Unfortunately for investors, the amount of data they need to digest can be overwhelming. Between earnings reports and economic data releases, it's easy for something to slip through the cracks.

For example, Feb. 17 marked the deadline for institutional investors with at least $100 million in assets under management to file Form 13F with the Securities and Exchange Commission. A 13F allows investors to track which stocks Wall Street's savviest money managers are buying and selling, including billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller of Duquesne Family Office .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Druckenmiller has an exceptional track record of crushing the broader market, with a touted annualized return of approximately 30% from 1981 to 2010. Unsurprisingly, Duquesne's 13F is among the most anticipated on Wall Street.

While there were certainly surprises in the latest 13F detailing Druckenmiller's fourth-quarter trading activity, such as the 29% reduction in chip fabricator Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing , it's Duquesne's billionaire boss's fascination with two of the hottest artificial intelligence (AI) stocks -- Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG) and Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) -- that made waves.

AI application stocks have billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller intrigued

AI represents the biggest technological leap forward since the advent and proliferation of the internet in the mid-1990s. By 2030, PwC's analysts believe it'll create more than $15 trillion in global economic value.

While graphics processing unit giant Nvidia and data-mining specialist Palantir Technologies have been among the biggest beneficiaries of the AI revolution, Druckenmiller sold his stakes in both companies some time ago. Nowadays, he favors Alphabet and Amazon.

During the fourth quarter, Duquesne's billionaire investor added 282,800 shares of Alphabet's Class A shares (GOOGL) and 300,870 shares of Amazon. This increased his stakes in both companies by 277% and 69%, respectively. It also marked the second consecutive quarter that Druckenmiller purchased shares in both companies.

Although Alphabet and Amazon have foundational operating segments -- Alphabet's Google accounts for approximately 90% of internet search market share, and Amazon's online marketplace dominates in the U.S. -- it's the incorporation of generative AI solutions and large language model capabilities into their cloud infrastructure service platforms that are fueling their growth.

Story Continues

For the fourth quarter, Alphabet reported 48% revenue growth for Google Cloud, the world's No. 3 cloud infrastructure services platform. Meanwhile, Amazon Web Services saw its sales growth reaccelerate to 24% when compared to the previous year. Although AI infrastructure investments have been eye-popping, Alphabet and Amazon are delivering tangible results that show this spending is worthwhile.

Both companies are also historically inexpensive relative to their forecasted cash flow in 2027. Alphabet is trading at 14.3 times next year's projected cash flow, while Amazon is even cheaper at 9.7 times estimated cash flow. To put these figures into context, Alphabet and Amazon are trading at 20% and 48% discounts to their respective average multiples to cash flow over the last five years.

Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller has a knack for spotting value hiding in plain sight. Alphabet and Amazon certainly appear to fit that definition.

While graphics processing unit giant Nvidia and data-mining specialist Palantir Technologies have been among the biggest beneficiaries of the AI revolution, Druckenmiller sold his stakes in both companies some time ago. Nowadays, he favors Alphabet and Amazon.

During the fourth quarter, Duquesne's billionaire investor added 282,800 shares of Alphabet's Class A shares (GOOGL) and 300,870 shares of Amazon. This increased his stakes in both companies by 277% and 69%, respectively. It also marked the second consecutive quarter that Druckenmiller purchased shares in both companies.

Although Alphabet and Amazon have foundational operating segments -- Alphabet's Google accounts for approximately 90% of internet search market share, and Amazon's online marketplace dominates in the U.S. -- it's the incorporation of generative AI solutions and large language model capabilities into their cloud infrastructure service platforms that are fueling their growth.

Story Continues

For the fourth quarter, Alphabet reported 48% revenue growth for Google Cloud, the world's No. 3 cloud infrastructure services platform. Meanwhile, Amazon Web Services saw its sales growth reaccelerate to 24% when compared to the previous year. Although AI infrastructure investments have been eye-popping, Alphabet and Amazon are delivering tangible results that show this spending is worthwhile.

Both companies are also historically inexpensive relative to their forecasted cash flow in 2027. Alphabet is trading at 14.3 times next year's projected cash flow, while Amazon is even cheaper at 9.7 times estimated cash flow. To put these figures into context, Alphabet and Amazon are trading at 20% and 48% discounts to their respective average multiples to cash flow over the last five years.

Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller has a knack for spotting value hiding in plain sight. Alphabet and Amazon certainly appear to fit that definition.

news.Title: 2 Beaten-Down AI Stocks to Buy Before the Next Bull Market
news.Content:
Although it's still officially in a bull market overall, the artificial intelligence (AI) investment sector may have something to say about that claim.

Many AI stocks are down more than 20% from their all-time highs, knocking them out of bull market territory on an individual stock basis. However, once geopolitical tensions stabilize and investors realize that AI spending will last for many more years, some of these stocks are slated to rocket higher.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Two that I have my eye on that are down at least 20% from their all-time highs are Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) and Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) . Microsoft is down more than 30%, while Broadcom is down nearly 25%. Each of these stocks has bright long-term prospects, and now is the time to scoop up these two before the market catches on.

Microsoft

Almost everyone is familiar with Microsoft's business, and the majority of office workers interact with Microsoft's software on a daily basis. Microsoft has also expanded its footprint into generative AI and is a key host for many leading generative AI companies. This is all done on its Azure cloud computing platform, and this business unit has seen impressive growth recently.

During the second quarter of fiscal year (FY) 2026, Azure's revenue rose 39% year over year. That helped lead Microsoft's overall revenue growth to 17% -- one of its best quarters in the past decade.

So, the question remains: Why is Microsoft stock selling off so much? The only good explanation I have is that Microsoft is being wrapped up in the general sell-off of software companies, as there is a notion that AI agents can rewrite most of this software, which would eliminate the need for many of Microsoft's subscriptions. I don't see this happening, as most of Microsoft's software is business-centric, and the cost of having this software fail is far greater than that of just paying Microsoft the subscription fee.

Regardless, Microsoft's sell-off has brought the stock to a point I can hardly believe. I like to use the operating price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio with Microsoft's stock because it ignores the effects of investment gains, which Microsoft has a lot of thanks to its OpenAI investment. If you utilize this metric, Microsoft is approaching the lowest point it has seen in the past decade.

Story Continues

Remember, Microsoft really isn't displaying any weakness in its business right now, so I think there has seldom been a better time than right now to buy Microsoft stock .

Broadcom

Broadcom is in a different boat than Microsoft. Broadcom's management has told investors that they expect monster growth over the next two years, but the market hasn't fully priced that growth into the stock yet.

Broadcom does a lot of different things as a business, but its most exciting segment is its custom AI chip business , which is rapidly taking off. Broadcom partners with AI hyperscalers to design a chip specifically suited to their needs, which can cut down on costs and improve performance.

In Q1 FY 2026 (ending Feb. 1), the division that custom AI chips are in saw its revenue increase 106% year over year to $8.4 billion. However, CEO Hock Tan told investors that its custom AI chips alone will generate $100 billion or greater revenue by the end of next year.

That's a huge jump and represents this division's revenue at least tripling in just two years. Over the past 12 months, Broadcom generated $68 billion in revenue, so its overall revenue is slated to skyrocket as well. Despite this, Broadcom's stock is off from its all-time high.

With the massive growth going on in the AI industry and Broadcom's product likely to capture some market share, I think now is the perfect time to load up on its stock.

news.Title: 《韓股》韓股重挫逾4% 韓元創17年新低
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】受到外資大舉賣超3.8兆韓元的衝擊，韓國股市31日大幅下跌224.84點或4.26%，收在5052.46點，連續第四個交易日收黑。同時，韓元匯價走貶，也創17年來最低。

韓媒指出，在這場開始於2月底，美以聯手空襲伊朗所引發的衝突，出現進一步升級下，市場避險動作加劇。美國時間30日，川普曾威脅說，如果伊朗不盡快跟美國達成和平協議，自己將徹底摧毀伊朗重要的石油中心哈爾克島，以及發電廠與油井。

不過，伊朗駁斥這些和平提議，因為不切實際、不合邏輯且要求過分。同時，伊朗議會安全委員會也批准一項法案，打算在荷姆茲海峽訂定收費制度。以色列總理納塔尼胡認為，這場戰爭已經實現過半的目標，但未給出戰爭結束的時間表。

分析師表示，伊朗危機加上油價上漲，推高股票的風險溢酬。因地緣政治風險，可能導致韓企獲利預估下修，故投資人最好抱持著謹慎的操作方式。個股中，三星電子大跌5.16%，SK海力士也重挫7.56%。同時，現代汽車大跌5.11%，起亞大跌4.16%，電池製造商LGES大跌3.78%，浦項鋼鐵也大跌3.06%。

韓元兌一美元在1,530.1價位附近，韓元大幅貶值14.4元，且連續第五個交易日走貶。韓元匯價創下自2009年三月九日以來最低，17年前，韓元一度貶至1549，當時全球曾有金融海嘯危機。

news.Title: 攻邊緣AI商機，磐儀擬辦理上限2千萬股私募案，引進策略投資人
news.Content:
【財訊快報／記者王宜弘報導】IPC廠磐儀(3594)搶攻邊緣AI商機，週二(31日)董事會決議辦理私募現金增資，以引進策略投資人，私募股數將不超過2千萬股，分兩次辦理。此外，週二董事會也通過去年度財報，全年稅後盈餘4666萬元，每股盈餘0.49元，獲利下滑主因為去年美國對等關稅以及新台幣匯率波動衝擊，營收則以20億元年增逾27%。

股利政策方面，磐儀董事會決議今年將配發0.4元現金股利，發放率81%，殖利率逾1%。

磐儀集團近年來背靠產業大咖，除了緯創(3231)、樺漢(6414)均是大股東，更與聯發科(2454)合資成立磐旭智能，劍指工業手持物聯網裝置市場；如今AI商機爆發，磐旭智能在聯發科扶植下全力導入ARM架構的虛擬伺服器(Virtual Cluster)產品，佈局企業級的資料中心與SOHO族市場，今年營運可望爆發，母公司磐儀將母以子貴，錦上添花。

因應AI市場的佈局，磐儀在既有基礎上，持續深化產業供應鏈的結盟，計畫透過私募引進策略夥伴，希望藉由策略投資人的技術、品牌知名度及全球市場通路，提升公司產品品質、降低生產成本，擴大市佔率，而私募額度將分兩次辦理，規模不超過2千萬股。

news.Title: 《日股》中東衝突持續 日經3月大跌
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】31日，日本日經225指數開低走低，盤中雖有短暫翻紅，但上漲力道不足走勢疲弱，最後收在51,063.72點，跌822.13點或1.58%，月線更是大跌超過13%，至少創下2010年五月以來最大單月跌幅。另外，東證收在3,497.86點，也跌44.48點或1.26%。

美媒指出，中東衝突升級之下，美股30日的科技族群大都走跌。中東30日，伊朗襲擊停在杜拜的一艘滿載原油的油輪。華爾街日報報導，美國總統川普表示，就算荷姆茲海峽還是封鎖，自己也願意結束對伊朗的軍事行動。

分析師認為，美國半導體相關類股大幅下挫，受到利空影響的日本股市，也出現拋售的壓力。如果盤勢不見好轉，日經指數將向下尋求50000點的重要關卡支撐。

半導體族群一片綠油油，愛德萬測試大跌2.8%，東京威力科創大跌4.2%，迪思科也大跌4.7%。人工智慧AI概念股中，軟銀重挫3.7%，藤倉暴跌超過9%，古河電氣工業大跌7%，住友電氣工業也下挫6.9%。

剔除掉波動較大的鮮食之後，日本首都三月核心CPI較去年同期上漲1.7%，低於二月的1.8%。若跟市場普遍預期的1.8%相比，東京三月物價漲幅較低，且創下自2024年四月以來最低。

日本政府表示，日本二月工業生產卻月減2.1%，是三個月來首次減少，主要是汽車表現不佳。當地15個產業中，高達12個的產出在二月減少，其中，汽車的產量繼一月成長8.1%之後，在二月則是月減3.6%。

日本二月失業率，從一月的2.7%降至2.6%，是過去七個月來首次出現改善，部分原因是之前主動離職的民眾，在上個月紛紛找到新工作。經季節性的因素調整後，日本二月就業人數小幅增加0.1%，達到6,827萬人。

news.Title: 聯發科攜手灣聲樂團　推動人文藝術扎根
news.Content:
（中央社記者張建中新竹2026年03月31日電）IC設計廠聯發科 (2454) 今天宣布，與灣聲樂團共同發起「台灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫，聯手將融合台灣文化靈魂的古典樂，帶入全台校園、社區與公共領域，共同守護在地文化，讓人文藝術在台灣各角落向下扎根。

聯發科發布新聞稿，董事長蔡明介說，聯發科技教育基金會長期投入台灣科技人才培育，並多年舉辦「智在家鄉」科技創新競賽，鼓勵以科技改善家鄉問題。

蔡明介表示，今年除了持續深耕科技人才培育，更將影響力擴大至在地人文的文化永續推廣，與灣聲樂團共同發起「台灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫，將音樂從南到北帶進家鄉的各角落。

聯發科指出，與灣聲樂團合作推動文化平權，期能促進文化近用性，讓藝文資源不侷限於都會或特定族群，能更深入台灣各地產生共鳴並向下扎根，讓更多民眾與青年學子都能享受音樂與文化的美好，進而啟發對於音樂的想像及喜愛。

聯發科表示，「台灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫初期將以市區快閃、校園交流與正式演出等一系列活動，北從馬祖，南至屏東。馬祖場系列活動將於4月16至19日展開，包含於馬祖南竿鐵堡等地進行快閃表演，至介壽國民中小學與學生交流互動，於梅石演藝廳舉辦免費音樂會，並與由馬祖東引在地青年組成的鹹味島合作社共同於東引忠誠門廣場舉行免費音樂會演出。

屏東場系列活動則於5月21至23日舉辦，除邀請金曲獎最佳台語歌手許富凱於屏東演藝廳共同演出，活動也將至南州國民中學、南州國民小學與學生交流互動，最終於潮州及南州地區快閃演出。

聯發科指出，「台灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」是與灣聲樂團共同辦理的永續活動，未來每年將鎖定2至3個縣市，持續累積更多在地共鳴。期望能有更多社會各界與藝文團體共同參與，加入永續在地文化的行列，一同支持台灣在地藝文與音樂教育。

news.Title: 元富期貨：台積電尾盤萬張賣單摜壓，台指今日以長黑K棒摜破季線
news.Content:
【財訊快報／編輯部】美股方面，美國總統川普30日聲稱與伊朗的談判取得進展，同時亦警告稱，若荷姆茲海峽未恢復通行，美方可能攻擊伊朗能源基礎設施，油價進一步走揚、加上記憶體與光通訊概念股下殺，衝擊費城半導體指數30日重挫逾4%，標普500指數和那斯達克指數連續三個交易日下跌，收在去年8月初以來最低水準，道瓊指數則在金融股支撐下微幅收紅。台指方面，台指今日呈現開低走低格局，盤中反彈力道較弱，顯示追價意願不足，午盤過後跌幅進一步擴大，權值王台積電尾盤出現萬張賣單摜壓。終場成交量7924.5億元，加權指數下跌795.17點、跌幅2.45%、收在31722.99點，整個3月狂瀉3691.5點，終止連3個月月線收紅的態勢。

綜合分析，台指今日以長黑K棒摜破季線，短期地緣政治風險具不確定性，清明連假前買盤縮手觀望，台指受消息面影響震盪恐加劇。未來一週，市場將主要聚焦於美國就業數據，同時也將密切關注邁入第二個月的美以伊戰爭進展。

news.Title: 《台股盤後》重挫795點、失守季線；月K翻黑
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-31 16:03:36 新聞中心 發佈

美股週一費半指數重挫影響下，台股今(31)日開低走低，雖美股電子盤在原油電子盤轉跌下呈漲勢，惟日經225指數半導體股卻未受激勵如TOKYO ELECTRON仍跌逾4%，再加上昨日美光重挫、今日原油期貨轉跌等，亦拖累先前強勢的塑化股如南亞等轉重挫、南亞科再呈弱勢，輔以台積電續在季線下震盪、台達電失守1400/聯發科失守1500關卡，盤面上19大類股中也僅造紙、水泥族群力守紅盤，導致加權指數終場收在31722.99點，下跌795.17點或2.45%，一舉摜破季線及32000點大關，成交量升至7924.51億元；月K翻黑、本月跌3691.50點或10.42%；季K連四紅、首季上漲2759.39點或9.53%。

週一國際油價進一步走揚、加上記憶體與光通訊概念股下殺，衝擊費城半導體(SOX)指數30日重挫逾4%，那斯達克指數、標準普爾500指數也受到壓抑。亞洲股市方面，日本半導體相關股重挫，拖累日經225指數一度暴跌逾1,300點，其後因美股指數期貨走揚，支撐日經指數跌幅收斂，終場跌1.58%；韓國KOSPI指數收盤重摔4.26%。

費城半導體指數週一重挫下，台股今日電子權值股普遍承壓，權王台積電開低走低，終場收在最低點1760元，下跌1.12%、續失守季線，欣興、致茂雙雙殺至跌停，智邦、台達電、日月光投控亦重挫逾7%。另外國內記憶體族群受美光股價重摔近10%拖累，南亞科、旺宏、宜鼎、宇瞻落入跌停，十銓、威剛、廣穎、品安、創見、凌航、群聯、華邦電連袂走跌。

在29大類股表現上，造紙類股上漲0.68%、水泥類股漲0.39%表現相對較佳，貿易百貨類股則維持平盤。造紙與水泥類股受惠於其傳統防禦特性、庫存整理題材，吸引部分避險資金進駐；貿易百貨類股走勢偏區間震盪，缺乏明顯方向性。弱勢股部分，以塑膠族群跌勢最重，其中昨日強勢股南亞下殺逾9%，台化、台苯、三芳、聯成同步下挫，主要係受到週二原油電子盤轉跌拖累。

三大法人動向方面，今日三大法人合計賣超986.64億元；其中，自營商賣超210.17億元，投信買超30.98億元，外資及陸資賣超807.46億元。

《台股盤後》收跌101點、日K翻黑，守住5日線

news.Title: The Best Quantum Computing Stock to Buy With $1,000 Right Now
news.Content:
While the world fixates on Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) chips fueling today's artificial intelligence (AI) boom, the company is quietly positioning itself as a foundational player in quantum computing.

With a modest sum of $1,000 to invest, Nvidia stands out as the smartest quantum AI play -- not because it builds the hardware, but because it already dominates the bridge between classical supercomputers and the quantum future.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Quantum computing represents a chance for Nvidia to transform its data center GPU empire into a hybrid computing platform that helps simulate, accelerate, and eventually integrate with true quantum systems -- unlocking exponential growth beyond raw chip sales.

Simulating quantum technology

Nvidia's CUDA software platform is already being extended into quantum toolkits, further locking in the company's developer base. These software libraries allow researchers to run quantum circuit simulations directly on Nvidia GPUs. In turn, these tools compress simulations that would take years to process on ordinary computers into hours.

This model helps developers test algorithms for next-generation breakthroughs in chemistry, logistics, and cryptography in real time. The lucrative theme here is that Nvidia's ecosystem is making quantum experimentation both scalable and affordable today -- eliminating the need to wait for fragile, capital-intensive quantum machines.

For investors, an investment in Nvidia already provides exposure to legitimate quantum AI progress, as the company already ships the hardware and software that power over 90% of the world's AI data centers .

Pure-play quantum start-ups like IonQ continue to burn cash on unproven physics. By contrast, Nvidia stands to profit from the simulation layer that every lab and corporation must use first.

Quantum AI opens new industry applications for Nvidia

Nvidia's Blackwell GPUs already train trillion-parameter models. Layering quantum-inspired optimization on top of these clusters can, in theory, reduce energy use and unlock optimization problems that classical AI still struggles with.

In terms of use cases, consider that quantum AI could conduct thorough portfolio rebalancing in milliseconds or help with molecular designs that lead to new drug discoveries overnight.

By controlling both sides-- classical computing acceleration and quantum simulation software -- Nvidia is quietly creating a flywheel that none of its rivals in the chip arena can match.

Story Continues

Nvidia's business model is resilient amid a volatile tech sector

Unlike speculative quantum developers that may take decades to deliver a commercially viable product, Nvidia already generates billions in profit from its data center business. This ongoing cash flow machine helps quietly fund the company's other ambitions, such as its quantum roadmap.

Nvidia trades at a forward P/E around 21 -- a multiple that only looks reasonable if you believe the company's future is limited to faster GPUs.

In reality, these trends illustrate that investors are not yet pricing in ancillary AI-driven growth from quantum simulation royalties, hybrid cloud integrations, and enterprise software licensing. These opportunities sit far beyond the next chip launch.

The market still sees Nvidia as a hardware vendor, but smart investors are recognizing that the company is becoming the de facto operating system for the quantum age. With $1,000, an investor can ride steady data center growth while still capturing asymmetric upside when quantum supremacy arrives.

news.Title: Beyond Nvidia: This Under-the-Radar AI Stock Is Up Nearly 300% Over the Past Year
news.Content:
Nvidia has been a great investment over the past few years, and I still think it's a great stock pick now. However, many investors are looking for alternatives or new ways to invest in the artificial intelligence (AI) space, and I think I've identified one that has been an absolute winner over the past year.

Micron (NASDAQ: MU) stock was up nearly 300% over the past year, although it has taken a bit of a break over the past few days. I think this stock looks like an excellent investment now, and the recent sell-off gives investors the perfect buying opportunity to get into this stock that could be a massive long-term winner.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Micron cannot meet demand

AI computing units have countless components that allow them to function. One of the most important is memory, which allows the computing unit to access stored information quickly. Without memory capacity, these chips don't function as effectively.

AI computing chips utilize a specific type of memory, known as high-bandwidth memory (HBM). Demand for this type is rapidly growing, and Micron expects the total HBM market opportunity to expand from $35 billion in 2025 to $100 billion by 2028.

The problem is, Micron cannot meet total demand right now. During its last conference call, Micron's management told investors that they only have enough capacity to meet between half and two-thirds of total demand. That's a big deal, and until Micron can get more production capacity up and running in 2027, there will continue to be a huge bottleneck. Even after increased capacity is up, there still may be a bottleneck due to rising computing unit demand.

One recent breakthrough that caused Micron's stock to sell off is Alphabet 's Google's TurboQuant algorithm. This reduces memory demand by six times, which at a glance may seem it destroys the Micron investment thesis.

However, this is only reducing memory demand on one part of the large language model. Furthermore, with this new innovation, companies may be free to improve their AI models because there is no memory crunch anymore. As a result, demand for memory will still be present; it will just be utilized in a different capacity.

Next quarter, Micron expects $33.5 billion in revenue, up from $23.9 billion this quarter and $13.6 billion the quarter before that. Micron is on an unreal growth trajectory, and with memory demand being in a large, multiyear rise, I think Micron is an excellent stock to invest in right now .

Story Continues

news.Title: 聯發科推動文化永續傳統，首度藝企合作發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」
news.Content:
【財訊快報／記者李純君報導】IC設計龍頭聯發科(2454)今日宣布首度藝企合作，與專精台灣傳統音樂的灣聲樂團，共同發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫，推動文化的永續傳承。聯發科表示，與灣聲樂團宣布共同發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫，聯手將融合臺灣文化靈魂的古典樂，帶入全臺校園、社區與公共領域，提升大眾的文化近用性，更結合雙方各自在科技教育與藝術推廣的專業影響力，共同守護在地文化，讓人文藝術在臺灣各角落向下扎根。

聯發科教育基金會長期致力於科技平權，積極縮短城鄉、性別與世代間的落差，與灣聲樂團多年來推動的「灣聲樂團．臺灣行腳」活動理念不謀而合，這次共同合作的「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」，將無國界的語言「音樂」送進台灣各角落，並期待將平權理念從科技領域延伸至文化領域，推動文化平權，促進文化近用性，讓藝文資源不侷限於都會或特定族群，而能更深入臺灣各地產生共鳴並向下扎根。

聯發科董事長蔡明介表示，自家的教育基金會長期投入臺灣科技人才培育，並多年舉辦「智在家鄉」科技創新競賽，鼓勵以科技改善家鄉問題，今年除了持續深耕科技人才培育，更將影響力擴大至在地人文的文化永續推廣，首度以藝企合作模式，與灣聲樂團共同發起『臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳』，將音樂從南到北帶進家鄉的各角落，並實踐企業回饋社會的理念。

同為本次活動共同主辦方的「灣聲樂團」，成立於2017年，是臺灣唯一以演出「臺灣音樂」為核心訴求的職業樂團。連年入圍金曲獎傳藝類獎項，創下音樂會平均售票率達九成的亮眼成績。除了在音樂廳演出，樂團最具代表性的品牌活動即是公益性質的《灣聲樂團 臺灣行腳》，透過「行動音樂廳」的精神深入臺灣各個角落，自2018年起已累積十四季、超過200場公益演出。

news.Title: 《台北股市》台股居3萬點高檔逾千檔股價破年線 QIC提四策略助力企業
news.Content:
【時報記者張佳琪台北報導】台股穩居3萬點之上，日成交金額8000億元幾乎成為常態，使得台股已晉升全球前十大的關鍵資本市場。不過，仍有1071家、高達50%的公司股價跌破年線，寬量國際(QIC)創辦人暨執行長李鴻基今(31)日對此表示，由AI主導的新資本世代，企業需換腦思考，應以「四大資本市場行動綱領」扭轉困境。

李鴻基表示，現階段台股已由10年前高殖利率、價值投資為主的導向，演進成以支援全球AI成長、高度資本支出為主導的「成長型資本市場」，在此情況下，約有1071家公司股價跌破年線，比重高達50%，值得關注。他指出，這些公司中不乏財務表現優質者，市值落後的核心主因在於「思維」問題，政策及企業決策者若不換腦思考，舊思維將難以進入AI主導的新資本世代。

台灣資本市場目前市值與日成交量分別居全球第7大與前10大。寬量國際(QIC)資料顯示，市場榮景下，全台上市櫃公司中50%跌破年線陷入「邊緣化」，在跌破年線的1071家公司中，有1029家為市值小於20億美元的小型股；有38家為市值20億至100億美元的中型股，包括統一超(2912)、中鋼(2002)、研華(2395)、鈊象(3293)、元太(8069)、上海商銀(5876)等。更有4家為市值大於100億美元的大型股，包括統一(1216)、長榮(2603)、台灣大(3045)、華碩(2357)。寬量國際(QIC)認為，這些公司即便擁有良好的財務體質，顯然需要找出新的資本市場論述方法，才能利用市場多樣性轉化為成長養分，與時俱進。

寬量國際(QIC)提出「四大行動綱領」，建議企業在台股新世代應調整的四大新戰略思維。首先是，找回公司成長道路，成長型的資本市場對於營收或獲利成長會付出溢價，大發現金股利已經不是主流，為成長做出的資本支出，和現金增資會得到市場的獎勵。

其次是公司應以論述長期願景，與設定具體的目標「未來式」為主流策略，而非側重財報解釋的「過去式」。因成長型市場與多變環境中，投資人更在乎未來發展藍圖，企業應及早提出改革遠景，並定期更新進程。

第三是定義「關鍵資訊」精準對接投資人。在資訊海量的AI世代，公司應定義並提供「關鍵資訊」，讓企業價值能有效率地被目標投資人看見，定期利用法說會及路演(Roadshow)提供一致「關鍵資訊」，是在多元市場中脫穎而出的重要方法論。

第四是以強化ESG行動來實質增加市值，而非只完成ESG報告書。QIC多年數據與方法論證明，ESG與市值的長期提升具有正向關鍵作用，透過積極落實ESG原則成為公司實際管理內容，且有計劃參與國際權威評比，並取得進步成績，是吸引對ESG關注的「被動基金」最容易管道。

寬量國際(QIC)也宣布，第19屆QIC CEO Week將於4月14日在新加坡盛大舉行。本屆論壇由工研院生醫所主辦、台北搖籃計畫贊助，以「AI, Biotech, and Beyond」為題，率領包含鴻勁(7769)、鈺祥(7909)、志聖(2467)、臻鼎(4958)、廣宇(2328)、億豐(8464)與穩懋(3105)等45家指標企業參與，並獨家引薦優質Pre-IPO企業，對接全球主權基金與長線資本。

news.Title: 《科技》小米POCO X8 Pro系列登台 搭聯發科晶片攻萬元手機市場
news.Content:
【時報記者王逸芯台北報導】小米宣布POCO X8 Pro系列在台上市，主打高續航與效能表現，並導入聯發科(2454)處理器，搶攻萬元級距市場。其中，「POCO X8 Pro Max」首度於X系列亮相，主打大電量與高效能配置；另同步推出鋼鐵人限定版與入門機款POCO M8 5G，擴大產品線布局。

POCO X8 Pro Max搭載8500mAh矽碳電池，為品牌目前最大電量設計，官方標榜可提供約兩天使用時間，並通過TUV SUD高循環電池認證，強調長期使用下的電池穩定性。POCO X8 Pro則配置6500mAh電池，在機身厚度與續航之間取得平衡。充電規格上，全系列支援100W快充與PPS協議，並首度在X系列導入27W反向充電功能，強化外出使用彈性。

處理器方面，POCO X8 Pro Max搭載聯發科天璣9500s，採3nm製程與全大核架構，跑分約308萬；POCO X8 Pro則採用天璣8500-Ultra，跑分逾228萬，鎖定中高階效能需求。整體定位上，系列主打在萬元價格帶提供接近旗艦等級的運算能力。

在系統與效能調校上，POCO導入「狂暴引擎」技術，透過動態調整CPU、GPU與記憶體頻率，兼顧效能與功耗。搭配3D環形冷泵散熱系統與LiquidCool技術，強化長時間運行下的穩定度。官方數據顯示，在《原神》等高負載遊戲中可支援最高120FPS顯示。

POCO X8 Pro Max配備6.83吋螢幕，Pro為6.59吋，皆採用M10面板，支援最高3500nits峰值亮度與3840Hz PWM調光，並取得TUV護眼認證。

影像方面，兩款機型皆搭載5000萬畫素主鏡頭與800萬畫素超廣角鏡頭，其中Max採用光影獵人600感光元件，強調低光環境拍攝能力。

系統則搭載Xiaomi HyperOS 3，整合AI功能，包括寫作輔助、即時翻譯與搜尋功能，並支援Google Gemini。「POCO X8 Pro Max」亦首度導入eSIM功能，提升跨國使用便利性。

此外，小米同步推出POCO M8 5G，鎖定入門市場，搭載Snapdragon 6 Gen 3處理器，配備120Hz螢幕與5000萬畫素相機，強化基本使用與娛樂體驗。

售價方面，POCO X8 Pro Max定價14,999元起，POCO X8 Pro為10,999元起，鋼鐵人限定版為13,999元；POCO M8 5G則為7,999元起，全系列將於4月1日開賣。

news.Title: 《半導體》不只做晶片！聯發科攜手灣聲樂團推音樂行腳扎根全臺
news.Content:
【時報記者王逸芯台北報導】聯發科(2454)與灣聲樂團宣布共同發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫，攜手將融合臺灣文化靈魂的古典音樂帶入全臺校園、社區與公共場域，提升大眾文化近用性。雙方並結合各自在科技教育與藝術推廣的專業影響力，共同守護在地文化，讓人文藝術在臺灣各角落向下扎根。

聯發科技教育基金會長期致力於科技平權，積極縮短城鄉、性別與世代間的落差，與灣聲樂團多年推動的「灣聲樂團．臺灣行腳」理念不謀而合。本次合作的「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」，將以音樂這項無國界的語言走入各地，並將平權理念由科技領域延伸至文化領域，推動文化平權與文化近用，讓藝文資源不再侷限於都會或特定族群，進一步深入各地產生共鳴，啟發更多民眾與青年學子對音樂的想像與熱愛。

聯發科董事長蔡明介表示，聯發科技教育基金會長期投入臺灣科技人才培育，並持續舉辦「智在家鄉」科技創新競賽，鼓勵以科技改善在地問題。今年除持續深耕科技教育外，也將影響力擴展至在地人文與文化永續推廣，首度以藝企合作模式與灣聲樂團共同發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」，將音樂從南到北帶進各地，實踐企業回饋社會的理念。

共同主辦的灣聲樂團成立於2017年，是臺灣唯一以演出「臺灣音樂」為核心訴求的職業樂團，連年入圍金曲獎傳藝類獎項，音樂會平均售票率達九成。除音樂廳演出外，樂團亦長期推動公益性質的《灣聲樂團 臺灣行腳》，以「行動音樂廳」形式深入各地，自2018年以來已累積14季、超過200場公益演出。

聯發科與灣聲樂團表示，「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」將作為長期推動的永續計畫，未來每年規劃前進2至3個縣市，持續累積在地共鳴，並期望邀請更多社會各界與藝文團體參與，共同支持臺灣在地藝文與音樂教育發展。

news.Title: 《台北股市》最慘血案！外資3月爆砍9682億 4月盛事拚反彈
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】權王台積電(2330)苦撐敗北，台股今日受累戰事延燒及清明連假前賣壓，盤中812及收盤795跌點，雙雙列居今年第四慘紀錄，更是拱手讓出生命線！本周下挫1389點呈現連2黑，3月累計狂殺3691點，更甚去年3月關稅戰干擾崩跌2,357點，創下史上第一大跌點，惟第一季仍上漲2373點，寫下史上最強Q1漲點。今日外資(不含外資自營商)賣超807.4551億元，投信買超30.9785億元，自營商賣超210.1668億元，三大法人賣超986.64億元。台股今日正式跌破季線，短線技術面延續偏弱調整趨勢，下檔支撐先看3/9低點，建議保守操作因應。

美伊開戰讓中東戰火燒不停，推升油價狂飆引發通膨疑慮，嚇壞主力、大戶等趁高落跑，外資今日賣超居今年第六大，自營商則砍出今年第五大紀錄！外資扮演空頭總司令，連4賣超達1889.58億元，3月更是大舉出脫9682.13億元，刷新史上最慘紀錄！國家隊則力挺台股，投信3月來買超963億元，擠進史上前五大；另八大公股截至30日累計3月買超高達2,025.76億元，已來到史上單月新高，宣示捍衛台股季線決心意味濃厚。

美軍在中東集結，川普對伊朗發出強硬警告，衝擊隔夜美股軟腳，晶片股遭血洗，台股受累清明長假將至市場更加保守，中場後記憶體指標南亞科(2408)、旺宏(2337)等殺跌停，矽光子、PCB、低軌衛星、高價千金股湧現拋售跌停潮，恐慌性賣壓狂瀉導致兵敗如山倒，拖累指數低見31705點、盤中大跌812.17點，創今年第四大，台積電(2330)尾盤遭狙擊收1760元最低，終場加權指數收在31722.99、下殺795.17點，寫今年第四大、史上第12慘，失守32K及季線護城河，成交值放大至7924.51億元。

美光重挫10%及傳出大陸通路市場價格鬆動雙重打擊，讓記憶體族群全線下沉潰不成軍，成拖垮台股的最後一根稻草，南亞科(2408)、旺宏(2337)、宜鼎(5289)、宇瞻(8271)、晶豪科(3006)、鈺創(5351)全鎖跌停，力積電(6770)、華邦電(2344)、創見(2451)、廣穎(4973)亦遭空頭血洗慘兮兮。

前期漲多的矽光子、低軌衛星、PCB明星股同遭狙擊成提款機，穩懋(3105)、南電(8046)、全新(2455)、欣興(3037)、IET-KY(4971)、光聖(6442)、昇達科(3491)、訊芯-KY(6451)、華星光(4979)、波若威(3163)、萊德光電-KY(7717)全數跌停躺平，出現少見的奇景。

專家表示，短期地緣政治風險仍具不確定性，台股恐震盪加劇，持續留意荷莫茲海峽通行狀況、油價動向及伊朗和談進展。法人則認為，若後續若恐慌指數、油價未再創高，有機會成為跌勢循環的尾聲。隨3月台股急速修正乖離後，4月後有望見到反彈契機，建議季線附近逢低加碼，「買黑不買紅」，看好太空經濟與低軌衛星、能源與重電族群成為戰火下的保命符。

時序即將進入第2季，4月優先關注第1季財報、台積電法說會、美國經濟數據表現及美國超級財報周，可擇優聚焦營收、財報表現優於預期的個股。建議操作嚴控持股水位、耐心等待市場落底訊號確立，保持風險控管。(編輯：龍彩霖)

news.Title: 三大法人賣超台股986.64億元
news.Content:
（中央社台北31日電）台股今天跌795.17點，收在31722.99點，成交值新台幣7924.5億元。三大法人外資及陸資賣超、投信買超、自營商賣超，合計賣超986.64億元。

自營商賣超210.16億元，投信買超30.97億元；外資及陸資賣超807.45億元，為歷史第13大賣超。1150331

news.Title: Citi Raises Target Price for Amazon.com (AMZN)
news.Content: Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN ) is one of the 10 best stocks that recently issued new debt . On March 25, Citi increased its price target on Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) from $265 to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Citi raised its price target for Amazon.com (AMZN) to $285 from $265, maintaining a Buy rating.
• Estimates for Amazon Web Services (AWS) were raised, driven by AI demand, with predictions of 28% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 and 37% in 2027.
• Wells Fargo maintained an Overweight rating with a $304 price target, highlighting compute capacity as a key success factor for AI-driven demand.
• Hyperscaler capacity is expected to double to 98GW, with annual CapEx reaching $860 billion by 2027, indicating significant cloud industry expansion.
• While Amazon is a strong investment, other AI stocks may offer greater upside potential and less downside risk.
news.Keywords: AWS, AI demand, price target, compute capacity, cloud growth

news.Title: 台積電開春新低 台股跌795點失守3萬2破季線
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者黃友柔,攝影陳柏誠

中東衝突未見緩和跡象，台股今（31）日開低走低跌破季線，記憶體族群持續逆風，成盤面最弱族群，包括旺宏、南亞科都以跌停作收；資金停泊在造紙、塑化產業，指數今天終場下跌795點，摜破季線收在31722點，成交量約7925億元，外資再賣台股807億，本月賣超9682億元 創單月歷史新高。儘管近期台股陷入整理，但加權指數累計第一季仍上漲2759點，漲幅達9.5%，東海大學財金系客座教授張錫認為，台股多頭格局不變，若跌破季線，反而提供投資人一個好買點。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「我想未來幾年台積電還是一個非常堅固的領頭羊，有它帶動，我想台灣要變空頭也不太容易。」

台股周二再度跳水，不過被稱為「多頭總司令」的張錫態度不變、依舊維持看多。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「這次還是因為戰爭的關係，造成很多的恐慌性，這個通膨油價我覺得還是一個比較短期的現象，遲早會回到基本正規的情況下。」

只是只要戰事一天不結束，投資人就會心慌慌，加權指數在3月的最後一個交易日，開低後跌勢一路擴大盤中重挫超過700點，失守32000點關卡。權值股表現疲弱之外，記憶體族群更持續淪為重災區，南亞科、旺宏打入跌停，威剛重挫超過9%，盤面上幾乎只剩下造紙類股逆勢上漲，華紙、寶隆攻上漲停價，塑化股永裕、地球也亮燈。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「其實這一次是美國先轉弱，標普也破季線，然後很多往年線靠攏這樣。但是我想台灣還是相對強，因為我們在供應鏈裡面算是相對安全，所以我覺得反正如果破季線是好買點，尤其是在AI供應鏈，在很多新的技術、很多供應台灣都扮演很重要的角色，所以我想相對是一個，就是至少跌時重質，我們是有質的，不是真的是那個比較虛的的上漲。」

張錫喊話台股有底氣，看好多頭格局沒有改變，尤其是以台積電獲利成長4成估計，在技術領先下，股價有望衝刺2500元，甚至明年3000元不是夢，更有機會帶動台股明年站上40000點。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「只是說個別公司，有些公司產能太高，或者是本益比太貴，或是鬼故事太多之類的，可能會做修正比較大；但是我覺得找好公司，基本面好的公司我覺得就是不用太擔心。」

當然最重要的還是要「基本面」，另外張錫也提醒，目前要擔心的就是通膨，雖然美國今年不至於會升息，但降息最多也就1碼。（記者黃友柔、陳柏誠／台北採訪報導）

news.Title: 台積電開春新低 台股跌795點失守3萬2破季線
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者黃友柔,攝影陳柏誠

中東衝突未見緩和跡象，台股今（31）日開低走低跌破季線，記憶體族群持續逆風，成盤面最弱族群，包括旺宏、南亞科都以跌停作收；資金停泊在造紙、塑化產業，指數今天終場下跌795點，摜破季線收在31722點，成交量約7925億元，外資再賣台股807億，本月賣超9682億元 創單月歷史新高。儘管近期台股陷入整理，但加權指數累計第一季仍上漲2759點，漲幅達9.5%，東海大學財金系客座教授張錫認為，台股多頭格局不變，若跌破季線，反而提供投資人一個好買點。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「我想未來幾年台積電還是一個非常堅固的領頭羊，有它帶動，我想台灣要變空頭也不太容易。」

台股周二再度跳水，不過被稱為「多頭總司令」的張錫態度不變、依舊維持看多。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「這次還是因為戰爭的關係，造成很多的恐慌性，這個通膨油價我覺得還是一個比較短期的現象，遲早會回到基本正規的情況下。」

只是只要戰事一天不結束，投資人就會心慌慌，加權指數在3月的最後一個交易日，開低後跌勢一路擴大盤中重挫超過700點，失守32000點關卡。權值股表現疲弱之外，記憶體族群更持續淪為重災區，南亞科、旺宏打入跌停，威剛重挫超過9%，盤面上幾乎只剩下造紙類股逆勢上漲，華紙、寶隆攻上漲停價，塑化股永裕、地球也亮燈。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「其實這一次是美國先轉弱，標普也破季線，然後很多往年線靠攏這樣。但是我想台灣還是相對強，因為我們在供應鏈裡面算是相對安全，所以我覺得反正如果破季線是好買點，尤其是在AI供應鏈，在很多新的技術、很多供應台灣都扮演很重要的角色，所以我想相對是一個，就是至少跌時重質，我們是有質的，不是真的是那個比較虛的的上漲。」

張錫喊話台股有底氣，看好多頭格局沒有改變，尤其是以台積電獲利成長4成估計，在技術領先下，股價有望衝刺2500元，甚至明年3000元不是夢，更有機會帶動台股明年站上40000點。

東海大學財金系客座教授 張錫：「只是說個別公司，有些公司產能太高，或者是本益比太貴，或是鬼故事太多之類的，可能會做修正比較大；但是我覺得找好公司，基本面好的公司我覺得就是不用太擔心。」

當然最重要的還是要「基本面」，另外張錫也提醒，目前要擔心的就是通膨，雖然美國今年不至於會升息，但降息最多也就1碼。（記者黃友柔、陳柏誠／台北採訪報導）

news.Title: 【Follow法人】外資甩賣0050逾10萬張居冠 暴砍台積電鴻海各2萬張...逆勢回補力積電
news.Content:
（下午4時50分更新外資買賣超）

台股今（31）日跌破季線，三大法人今日賣超986.64億元；外資續殺紅眼，大舉賣超807.46億元；3月以來賣超金額高達9682.13億元，寫史上單月第一大賣超紀錄。外資主要賣超權值股台積電（2330）和鴻海（2317）各兩萬張，並持續出脫0050逾10萬張。此外，逆勢回補力積電（6770）。

外資今日賣超前三大：元大台灣50、台新新光金、群益台灣精選高息

外資重砍元大台灣50（0050）10萬4,126張居冠，為連續第26日賣超，賣超金額為75.72億元。今日甫公布配息的群益台灣精選高息（00919）3萬4,114張，呈連4賣；主動復華未來50（00991A）賣超3萬3,650張。

另外，外資同步賣超鴻海2萬1,639張，以及台積電2萬437張，賣超金額分別達41億元、362.13億元。

外資今日買超前三大：復華富時不動產、力積電、群益優選非投等債

記憶體族群再淪空頭狙殺對象，外資則逆勢買超力積電2萬5,365張，由賣轉買；並敲進華邦電（2344）5,077張，同樣由賣轉買。

根據證交所公布籌碼動向，三大法人今日合計賣超986.64億元，其中外資續砍807.46億元，累計4個交易日調節台股達1886.58億元。

投信續站買方，買超30.98萬元。自營商合計賣超210.17億元，其中自行買賣部分賣超16.67億元，避險方面賣超193.49億元。

中東戰事僵局持續，衝擊國際股市表現，台股盤中一度下殺812點，來到31,705.99點。終場下跌795.17點，為台股第12大收盤跌點，跌破季線並失守32000點關卡，累計3月重挫達3691.5點。

王詩婷／財經特派記者，主跑勞動部、半導體、生技等
